title: Quantum Gravity & Loop Quantum Gravity

analysis.object: T207: EML depth analysis of quantum gravity and LQG

spin_networks.description: LQG spin networks: graphs with SU(2) labels
spin_networks.reason: Combinatorial structure — edge labels are discrete integers (j = 0, 1/2, 1, ...)

area_operator.reason: √(j(j+1)) is a square root — algebraic function of discrete j values

volume_operator.description: Volume eigenvalues — determinant of E-field operators
volume_operator.reason: Polynomial + square root structure; discrete spectrum

black_hole_entropy.reason: Logarithmic corrections: S = S_BH + (1/2)ln(A) + ... — pure EML-1 (logarithm)

spin_foam_amplitude.description: Transition amplitude W = Σ_f A(f) over spin foams
spin_foam_amplitude.reason: Oscillatory path integral over discrete geometries — sum of exp(iS) terms

planck_scale_cutoff.reason: Single discrete quantity — no continuous variable

hawking_temperature.reason: Rational function of mass M; no oscillation

graviton_scattering.reason: Perturbation series diverges — no finite EML description of UV completion

analysis.shadow_depth_theorem_check: LQG Selberg-class analog: spin foam amplitude = oscillatory sum → shadow=3. SDT holds for quantum gravity amplitudes where Ramanujan-type bounds apply.

analysis.verdict: LQG: discrete geometry EML-0/2; spin foams EML-3; UV completion EML-∞
analysis.theorem: T207: QG-LQG Depth Map — area/volume EML-2, spin foam EML-3, graviton UV EML-∞

key_theorem: T207: QG-LQG Depth Map (S486). Spin networks: EML-0 (discrete labels). Area/volume eigenvalues: EML-2 (√j(j+1)). BH entropy corrections: EML-1 (logarithm). Spin foam amplitudes: EML-3 (oscillatory path integral). Graviton UV completion: EML-∞ (perturbation series diverges). Key revelation: the discrete-to-continuous transition in LQG = EML-0→EML-3 jump.
rabbit_hole_log: Spin network labels: j ∈ {0,1/2,1,...} → EML-0 (pure combinatorics)
Spin foam: Σ exp(iS_Regge) → EML-3 (oscillatory sum over geometries)
T207: Quantum gravity depth map — discrete structure meets oscillatory dynamics
